question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER cause a fund freeze by exploiting the fact that the EIP-712 domain separator does not include a salt or version, so a signature created for EcrecoverRatifier on chain C is valid for any EcrecoverRatifier at the same address on any chain with the same chainId, under the precondition that two chains have the same chainId (e.g. testnets or forks), triggering the call sequence [sign offer on chain C1 with chainId=1, replay on chain C2 with chainId=1 -> isRatified passes -> unauthorized fill on C2], violating the invariant that a signature must be bound to a specific chain, causing scoped impact: cross-chain signature replay enabling unauthorized fills or fund freeze on the target chain? Proof idea: vm.chainId(1) on two test instances; assert signature from one is accepted by the other.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER cause a fund freeze by exploiting the fact that isRatified does not check offer.start <= block.timestamp (that check is in Midnight.take), so a ratifierData with a valid signature for a not-yet-started offer would pass isRatified but fail in take, under the precondition that an offer has offer.start > block.timestamp and taker submits it, triggering the call sequence [take(not_started_offer, ratifierData, ...) -> Midnight.take checks block.timestamp < offer.start -> OfferNotStarted revert before isRatified is called], violating the invariant that not-started offers must not be fillable, causing scoped impact: confirms that start enforcement is in Midnight not EcrecoverRatifier, so a bypass of Midnight's start check would allow premature fills? Proof idea: differential test; assert OfferNotStarted revert occurs before isRatified is called.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER cause a fund freeze by exploiting the fact that isRatified checks _signer == offer.maker OR isAuthorized[offer.maker][_signer], so if maker is a contract that always returns true for isAuthorized (e.g. a permissive smart contract wallet), any ecrecover result (including address(0) if not blocked) would pass the authorization check, under the precondition that offer.maker is a contract with permissive isAuthorized, triggering the call sequence [take(offer_with_contract_maker, abi.encode(any_sig, root, leafIndex, proof), ...) -> isRatified -> ecrecover returns any_address -> isAuthorized[contract_maker][any_address] returns true -> passes], violating the invariant that only authorized signers must be able to ratify offers, causing scoped impact: unauthorized fills of contract maker's offers, draining credit or collateral? Proof idea: deploy permissive contract maker; assert any signature passes isRatified.
